_about: Local historical alert catalogs for archival cross-matching (F4). The pipeline analyzes O1-O3 data (2015-2020) — settled history — so alerts are looked up in these bundled lists, not queried from live alert APIs. Times OUTSIDE a catalog's coverage window return status 'skipped' (not a fake negative). See ligo-mcp/REVIEW_FINDINGS.md and IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md.

snews.completeness: COMPLETE for the LIGO observing era. SNEWS has issued ZERO real supernova alerts since it began; the last galactic core-collapse supernova was SN 1987A. An empty alert list is the correct, complete historical record for O1-O4.
snews.source: SNEWS collaboration (snews.bnl.gov) — no public alerts on record for 2015-present.

icecube.completeness: POPULATED (July 10, 2026) from the GCN/AMON notice archives — the full public realtime-alert record (217 alerts, Apr 2016 through 2026-07-08). A 'no alert' within coverage is now an authoritative negative for the realtime program. Two caveats: (1) pre-2016 archival reconstructions (IceCat-1 covers 2011+) are NOT included — times before Apr 2016 return 'skipped', which is why coverage_start matters; (2) the rare alert retracted later via GCN Circular (not machine-readable) may still appear here — an escalation on a retracted alert is acceptable, it goes to human review anyway.
icecube.source: GCN/AMON notice archives: amon_ehe_events.html + amon_hese_events.html (2016-2019) + amon_icecube_gold_bronze_events.html (2019-present). Highest revision kept per event.
